homepage.heroSubline: Custom bath remodels designed for historic homes and modern lifestyles. Serving Cambridge, Newton, Brookline, and Quincy with precision craftsmanship.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Legacy Bath Remodel began with a mission to preserve Boston's architectural heritage while updating homes for contemporary living. Our team of local craftsmen understands the unique challenges of working with historic brownstones, triple-deckers, and waterfront properties.

We approach every project with a balance of historical sensitivity and innovative design. From preserving original tilework in Back Bay homes to installing heated floors for harsh New England winters, we tailor solutions to your space and lifestyle.

As a proud member of the Greater Boston Home Builders Association, we're committed to sustainable practices that honor our community. We source materials from local suppliers whenever possible and prioritize water-efficient fixtures for our coastal environment.

valueProps.title: Historic Expertise
valueProps.description: Specialized in preserving original features while modernizing functionality in Boston's aging homes.

valueProps.title: Climate-Adapted
valueProps.description: Solutions designed for freeze-thaw cycles, salt air corrosion, and ice dam prevention.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: Deep understanding of Boston's building codes, neighborhood aesthetics, and material suppliers.

valueProps.title: Transparent Process
valueProps.description: Clear timelines and communication from initial consultation to final walkthrough.

homepage.ctaSubline: Schedule a consultation to discuss how we can bring your vision to life.

services.name: Historic Bath Preservation
services.shortDescription: Restoring original charm while adding modern functionality to Boston's vintage bathrooms.
services.description:
Boston's historic homes often feature irreplaceable architectural details that deserve careful preservation. Our team specializes in restoring original clawfoot tubs, hex tile flooring, and vintage fixtures while discreetly updating plumbing and electrical systems.

We carefully document existing conditions before work begins, using reversible installation methods whenever possible. For Back Bay brownstones and South End townhomes, we source reproduction moldings and period-appropriate hardware to maintain architectural integrity. Modern comforts like discreet radiant heating and low-flow plumbing are integrated without compromising historic character.
process: Step 1: Historic assessment and documentation of existing features
Step 2: Custom solution planning with preservation specialists
Step 3: Gentle demolition protecting salvageable elements
Step 4: Installation with reversible techniques and period materials
benefits: Modern functionality hidden in vintage aesthetics
Specialized craftsmen trained in restoration techniques

faq.question: Can you match my original 1920s subway tile?
faq.answer: Yes, we work with local artisans who recreate historic tile patterns and glaze colors. For extensive projects, we can source original salvaged materials from Boston architectural reclaimers.

faq.question: How do you handle lead paint in old bathrooms?
faq.answer: Our team is EPA-certified in lead-safe practices. We contain the work area with plastic barriers and use HEPA vacuums, following all Massachusetts lead abatement protocols.

faq.question: Will a historic remodel take longer than new construction?
faq.answer: Typically adding 1-2 weeks for careful demolition and custom fabrication. We build this into our timeline upfront to avoid surprises.

services.name: Nor'easter-Proof Bathrooms
services.shortDescription: Weather-resistant designs that stand up to Boston's harsh winters and coastal moisture.
services.description:
New England's freeze-thaw cycles and salty coastal air demand specially engineered bathroom solutions. We install moisture-resistant wall systems behind tiles to prevent mold in humid summers, and use anti-fracture membranes to protect against winter's temperature swings.

For homes near the harbor, we specify marine-grade hardware that resists salt corrosion. Ice dam prevention starts with proper insulation and venting behind shower walls. Our heated floor systems provide comfort during bitter Cambridge winters while reducing condensation that damages finishes. Every material is tested for durability in our local climate.
process: Step 1: Climate assessment of your home's exposure
Step 2: Selection of weather-appropriate materials
Step 3: Installation with moisture barriers and thermal breaks
Step 4: Final testing of drainage and ventilation systems

faq.question: What flooring works best for snowy winters?
faq.answer: Radiant-heated porcelain tile provides warmth while resisting moisture damage from tracked-in snow. We install it with slope for quick drying in mudroom bathrooms.

faq.question: How do you prevent pipes from freezing?
faq.Answer: We reroute vulnerable plumbing away from exterior walls when possible, and install frost-proof sillcocks with proper insulation in Newton's coldest neighborhoods.

services.name: Brownstone Bath Remodels
services.shortDescription: Space-maximizing designs for Back Bay and South End's iconic narrow layouts.
services.description:
Boston's historic brownstones present unique spatial challenges with their long, narrow footprints and limited plumbing access. Our designs create the illusion of space through strategic mirror placement, vertical storage, and continuous flooring that visually expands the room.

We specialize in fitting full amenities into compact footprints - installing corner showers with built-in shelving, pedestal sinks that preserve floor space, and water-saving fixtures that don't sacrifice performance. For garden-level units, we address moisture issues with proper waterproofing before installing finishes. Every design considers the brownstone's original sightlines and proportions to maintain architectural harmony.
process: Step 1: Measuring and 3D modeling of your unique space
Step 2: Custom storage and fixture solutions
Step 3: Careful demolition in tight quarters
Step 4: Installation with attention to original architectural details

faq.question: Can you add a second bathroom to my brownstone?
faq.answer: Yes, we've created powder rooms under staircases and stacked bathrooms by coordinating with structural engineers familiar with Back Bay's building constraints.

faq.question: How do you handle low water pressure in old buildings?
faq.answer: We install pressure-boosting systems specifically designed for historic properties, with discreet placement to maintain aesthetics.

services.name: Universal Design Bathrooms
services.shortDescription: Accessible designs that blend seamlessly with Boston's historic homes.
services.description:
Creating bathrooms that accommodate all life stages requires thoughtful design, especially in Boston's older homes with space constraints. We install barrier-free showers with discreet linear drains, comfort-height toilets with traditional styling, and lever handles that complement vintage aesthetics.

For Newton's multigenerational households, we design flexible spaces that can adapt over time - installing reinforcement for future grab bars behind beautiful wainscoting, or creating curbless transitions that work with wheelchairs while maintaining elegant finishes. Every solution balances accessibility needs with the architectural integrity of your home.
process: Step 1: Needs assessment and future planning
Step 2: Space planning for clear floor areas
Step 3: Installation with hidden structural supports
Step 4: Testing of all accessibility features
benefits: Prepares your home for aging in place

faq.question: Can accessible design work in my small Beacon Hill bathroom?
faq.answer: Absolutely. We've installed space-saving solutions like wall-hung vanities and pocket doors in some of Boston's most compact historic bathrooms.

faq.question: Do accessible showers look different?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. We create beautiful curbless showers with hidden slopes and matching floor tiles throughout for a seamless look.

services.name: Luxury Primary Suites
services.shortDescription: Spa-inspired retreats for Boston's waterfront condos and Newton estates.
services.description:
For Boston's luxury properties, we craft bathroom sanctuaries that rival high-end spas. Our designs incorporate radiant-heated stone floors, steam shower systems with chromatherapy lighting, and custom vanities with integrated refrigeration for skincare products.

In waterfront condos, we maximize ocean views with strategically placed soaking tubs and frameless glass enclosures. For Newton's larger homes, we create his-and-hers dressing areas with tailored storage. Every detail considers both aesthetics and functionality - from silent-closing drawers to humidity-sensing ventilation that protects your investment.
process: Step 1: Lifestyle assessment and wishlist development
Step 2: Material selection and technology integration
Step 3: Precision installation of high-end finishes
Step 4: Systems programming and owner training

faq.question: What luxury features are popular in Boston homes?
faq.answer: Our clients love towel warming drawers for cold mornings, and integrated sound systems that withstand bathroom humidity while providing spa ambiance.

faq.question: Can you match my existing high-end finishes?
faq.answer: Yes, we have relationships with premium suppliers and can source matching materials from the same quarries or manufacturers.

reviews.name: Eleanor R.
reviews.text: Legacy transformed our 1890s South End brownstone bathroom while preserving the original clawfoot tub and hex tiles. They sourced perfect matches for missing tiles and discreetly updated all the plumbing. The shower looks like it's always been there - just with modern water pressure!

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: After our Quincy bathroom flooded during a nor'easter, Legacy rebuilt it with waterproofing that could survive a hurricane. The heated floor makes winter mornings bearable, and the marine-grade fixtures still look perfect after two salty winters.

reviews.name: Sophia K.
reviews.text: Somehow made our tiny Beacon Hill bathroom feel twice as big. The custom floating vanity provides storage without crowding the space, and the extra-tall showerhead makes the low ceiling less noticeable. They worked around our odd plumbing without compromising design.

reviews.name: David L.
reviews.text: Legacy created an accessible bathroom for my mother that doesn't look institutional. The beautiful wainscoting hides grab bar reinforcements, and the curbless shower is safer but still elegant. They understood exactly how to blend function with our Newton home's traditional style.

reviews.name: Olivia C.
reviews.text: Our Seaport condo bathroom is now a legitimate spa. The backlit onyx shower walls and teak flooring create such a serene atmosphere. The team handled all the high-end finishes with care and programmed the smart controls to be actually usable (not just flashy).

reviews.name: Henry W.
reviews.text: Restored our Cambridge Victorian's original mosaic floor that three other contractors said was beyond repair. Their tile specialist spent days carefully cleaning and re-grouting by hand. The shower conversion maintained all the historic charm while adding modern functionality.

service-areas.title: Boston Area Bathroom Remodeling
service-areas.description: Serving Cambridge, Newton, Brookline, Quincy with bath remodels designed for New England homes and climate.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Bath Remodel is proud to serve the historic city of Cambridge, MA, where Victorian homes near Harvard University require specialized care to preserve their charm while modernizing functionality. Our team understands the unique challenges of remodeling bathrooms in older homes, from updating plumbing to matching period-appropriate fixtures. We also account for the harsh New England climate, ensuring your new bathroom can withstand cold, snowy winters and Nor'easters while remaining energy-efficient.
serviceAreas.localContext: Cambridge is known for its Victorian-era homes and academic atmosphere, with Harvard University and MIT anchoring the city's vibrant culture.

serviceAreas.content: In Newton, MA, Legacy Bath Remodel specializes in transforming upscale Colonial and Tudor homes with luxurious, high-end bathroom renovations. Our team respects the architectural integrity of these elegant properties while integrating modern amenities like heated floors and smart showers to combat the cold, snowy winters and Nor'easters. We work closely with Newton homeowners to create timeless designs that enhance both comfort and property value.
serviceAreas.localContext: Newton is an affluent suburb featuring stately Colonial and Tudor-style homes, known for its excellent schools and quiet, tree-lined neighborhoods.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Bath Remodel excels in updating bathrooms in Brookline, MA, where early 20th-century apartment buildings and homes demand careful attention to detail. We specialize in maximizing space in smaller bathrooms while preserving the historic character of these properties. Our weather-resistant materials and insulation upgrades ensure your bathroom stays cozy during the region's cold, snowy winters and Nor'easters.
serviceAreas.localContext: Brookline is a densely populated town with a mix of early 1900s-1930s apartments and single-family homes, offering a blend of urban and suburban living.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Bath Remodel is a trusted name for bathroom renovations in Quincy, MA, where 1950s-60s Cape Cod and Colonial homes benefit from our expertise in mid-century modernization. We help homeowners update outdated layouts while maintaining the classic New England charm of these properties. Our durable, moisture-resistant materials are ideal for Quincy's cold, snowy winters and Nor'easters, ensuring long-lasting beauty and functionality.
serviceAreas.localContext: Quincy is a coastal city with a mix of mid-century Cape Cod and Colonial homes, offering a suburban feel with easy access to Boston.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Bath Remodel serves Somerville, MA, where historic triple-deckers and modern condos alike require innovative bathroom solutions. Our team is skilled at transforming tight spaces into efficient, stylish retreats, perfect for Somerville's dense urban environment. We also prioritize weatherproofing to handle the cold, snowy winters and Nor'easters common in the area.
serviceAreas.localContext: Somerville is a vibrant, densely populated city known for its eclectic mix of historic homes and modern developments.
